results.compiled_code:
SELECT
    transaction_id,
    customer_id,
    store_location,
    product_category,
    item_count,
    -- Cast decimal to float or keep as is; ClickHouse handles Decimal(38,9) fine, but casting keeps it lightweight
    CAST(purchase_amount AS Float64) as purchase_amount,
    -- Use the pre-parsed timestamp column Airbyte provided
    transaction_timestamp
FROM `retail_analyticsdb`.`raw_transactions`

results.compiled_code:
SELECT
    store_location,
    product_category,
    COUNT(transaction_id) as total_orders,
    SUM(item_count) as total_items_sold,
    SUM(purchase_amount) as total_revenue,
    ROUND(AVG(purchase_amount), 2) as average_order_value,
    CAST(transaction_timestamp AS Date) AS transaction_date,
FROM `retail_analyticsdb`.`stg_transactions`
GROUP BY 
    store_location, 
    product_category,
    transaction_timestamp
